author: phase-4-coordinator
content: Updated the crate-level, module-level, and per-trait docstrings to accurately describe what the conversions actually do: every path (NumPy/Arrow/Polars in both directions) copies the data buffer. The new docs include a table enumerating the six paths and the specific reason each one copies, a short explanation of what true zero-copy would require, and a note that the copies are still single-memcpy-per-conversion. No runtime behavior change — this is a documentation fix to stop misleading users about allocation behavior. Trait docstrings on AsFerray and IntoNumPy are also updated so they no longer claim zero-copy semantics. Also fixed pre-existing -3.14 ~= PI clippy lints in three test fixtures while here.
